In the [1º turno](/en/glossary#primeiro-turno), Lula 39% × Flávio 35% repeats identical reading from April 11, indicating that declared vote has not yet absorbed the scandal. [Polymarket](/en/glossary#polymarket), however, widens the Lula gap to +15.65pp (vs +10pp at the April 14 close) and STF impeach surges to 12.75% (↑8.25pp in 24h from the reversal). Market priced in Flávio erosion at a magnitude greater than the survey registers."
---
# May 15, 2026

> Friday (May 15) brings the national [Datafolha](/en/glossary#datafolha) (n=2,004) — first field survey already covering the complete window of the Flávio-Vorcaro audio. In the [1º turno](/en/glossary#primeiro-turno), **Lula 39% × Flávio 35% repeats identical reading from April 11**, indicating that declared voting has not yet absorbed the scandal. The [Polymarket](https://polymarket.com/event/brazil-presidential-election), however, **widens the Lula gap to +15.65pp** (vs +10pp at May 14 close) and [STF impeach spikes to 12.75% (↑8.25pp in 24h from reversal)](https://polymarket.com/event/any-brazil-stf-justice-removed-by-impeachment-before-2027). Market priced in Flávio erosion in magnitude greater than the survey registers.

## 1. Prediction market

On [Polymarket](https://polymarket.com/event/brazil-presidential-election) at 11:00 BRT on May 15, **Lula expands to 44.50% (↑1.50pp vs May 14 close)** and **Flávio Bolsonaro continues yielding to 28.85% (↓4.15pp in 18h)**. The gap between the two rises from +10.00pp Lula at May 14 close to **+15.65pp Lula on May 15 morning** — acceleration of the difference that reverses Flávio's partial recovery observed the previous morning. Accumulated in 24h post-arrest of Henrique Vorcaro: Flávio yields 4.50pp additional.

The **third way has bifurcated direction**. [Renan Santos (Missão/MBL) rises to 9.45% (↑2.45pp)](https://polymarket.com/event/brazil-presidential-election) and surpasses Zema by a wide margin in the presidential. [Romeu Zema (Novo) yields to 6.60% (↓2.40pp)](https://polymarket.com/event/brazil-presidential-election) — returns part of the partial recovery registered at May 14 close (9.00%). [Caiado (PSD) rises slightly to 1.55% (↑0.20pp)](https://polymarket.com/event/brazil-presidential-election). [Michelle Bolsonaro rises slightly to 2.85% (↑0.85pp)](https://polymarket.com/event/brazil-presidential-election) — substitution narrative maintains positive vector, but at slower pace than the May 14 spike. Haddad stable at 2.35% (↓0.10pp).

In the **2nd place market**, [Flávio yields to 58.50% (↓6.50pp vs 65.00%)](https://polymarket.com/event/brazil-presidential-election-first-round-2nd-place) — market reduces probability of Flávio even as 2nd place. Lula stable at 15.50%. Renan rises to 6.30% and Zema doubles to 6.20% (↑3.20pp) — Renan and Zema are tied in this market for the first time in the series.

The **3rd place market** reorganizes: [Zema maintains top with 39.50%](https://polymarket.com/event/brazil-presidential-election-first-round-3rd-place), Renan stable at 32.00% and Caiado recovers to 18.00% (↑5.00pp vs 13.00%) — Caiado returns to competing for 2nd position in the 3rd place market. Michelle appears 4th with 6.20%.

The **STF minister impeachment market** [spikes to 12.75% (↑4.75pp vs 8.00%)](https://polymarket.com/event/any-brazil-stf-justice-removed-by-impeachment-before-2027), accumulating 8.25pp of gains in 24 hours from reversal initiated at May 14 close. With no clear exogenous triggering event today (no new [STF](/en/glossary#stf) ruling nor amnesty [PEC](/en/glossary#pec) on agenda), the reading is market repricing the institutional trajectory. In the [Senate, PL recovers 78.00% (↑5.00pp vs 73.00%)](https://polymarket.com/event/next-brazil-senate-election-most-seats-won) — the spread fell to [Republicanos](/en/glossary#republicanos), which resurges in market at 11.65% (vs 1.05% on May 14 morning). [União Brasil](/en/glossary#uniao-brasil) at 4.90%, [PSD](/en/glossary#psd) at 4.20%, [PT](/en/glossary#pt) at 2.95%. In [2026 inflation](https://polymarket.com/event/brazil-annual-inflation-2026), the 5.00-5.49% interval rises to 25.85% (↑1.70pp) and the 7.00%+ recedes to 10.65%.

## 2. What the institutes registered

The day is dominated by the **national [Datafolha](/en/glossary#datafolha) published on May 15**, with sample of 2,004 in-person interviews between May 12-14 ([TSE](/en/glossary#tse) register BR-00290/2026, cost R$ 307,641.60). In the main [1º turno](/en/glossary#primeiro-turno) scenario, [Lula has 39% × Flávio 35% — identical to the Datafolha of April 11](https://revistaforum.com.br/politica/pesquisa-datafolha-flavio/). In the [2º turno](/en/glossary#segundo-turno), **Flávio appears numerically ahead with 46% × 45% of Lula** ([Empate técnico](/en/glossary#empate-tecnico) maintained), while Lula defeats [2º turno](/en/glossary#segundo-turno) against Caiado (45×42) and against Zema (45×42). In the spontaneous question, Flávio rises from 12% to 16% (↑4pp) — sign of greater salience without changing stated estimated intention.

Two other surveys arrive the same day in different directions. The **Vox Brasil**, with interviews completed before the Vorcaro case according to Bahia Notícias, shows Flávio ahead in [2º turno](/en/glossary#segundo-turno) with 43.8% × 40.2% of Lula (Poder360 coverage, Revista Oeste, GP1). Meanwhile, **[AtlasIntel](/en/glossary#atlasintel) tracking**, released by Exame and VEJA, indicates Lula 7 points ahead of Flávio in [2º turno](/en/glossary#segundo-turno) after audio revelation (additional BPMoney coverage). The three institutes tell three stories compatible with chronology: Datafolha captures full window but shows same reading as April; Vox has old data that no longer holds; [AtlasIntel](/en/glossary#atlasintel) tracking captures more pronounced effect in short term.

**Next publications** registered with [TSE](/en/glossary#tse): Veritá on May 16 (n=3,330, field May 11-15); QUALITTA and PARLA MENTORS on May 16; ECM on May 17; [AtlasIntel ×2 on May 19 (n=1,200 + n=5,000)](https://eleitor.tse.jus.br/sip/sip-pesquisas); Real Time Big Data and Vox Brasil on May 20; and **VETOR ARROW on May 20 with sample n=9,000** — the largest of the window. State-level: Poder360 reports on May 15 that Lula appears ahead of Flávio in [1º turno](/en/glossary#primeiro-turno) and [2º turno](/en/glossary#segundo-turno) in Maranhão.## 3. What the press covered

The **dynamics of political articulation around Flávio's candidacy** occupy the central axis of coverage. Folha de S.Paulo reports that "Centrão reconsiders alliance with Flávio Bolsonaro after audio recordings showing closeness to Vorcaro" and that "Bolsonarists see Tarcísio distant and want greater support for Flávio". G1 covers that "Tebet sees Flávio's candidacy unsustainable after audio recordings". Folha itself registers that "Flávio Bolsonaro bets on Master CPI to counter wear-and-tear, but allies see minimal chances" and that Flávio stated: "I have nothing to justify to anyone" about the conversations with Vorcaro. Brasil de Fato headlines with the thesis that "If he continues as a candidate, he will be destroyed by October" and questions whether it is time for Flávio to withdraw.

The **BBC's interpretive reading** of the audio's effects is the analytical highlight of the day: "Recording of Flávio Bolsonaro with Vorcaro boosts Caiado and Zema, but does not benefit Lula: 'Voters looked to the side'" — a reading that partially reconciles [Datafolha](/en/glossary#datafolha) stability in the [1º turno](/en/glossary#primeiro-turno) with [Polymarket](/en/glossary#polymarket) movement. The BBC also brought the thesis from British magazine The Economist that "Flávio Bolsonaro's candidacy is threatened by 'bombshell' leak" and provided a retrospective of "What Flávio Bolsonaro said about [Banco Master](/en/glossary#banco-master) and Daniel Vorcaro in recent months".

In **Lula's response**, Folha covers that "Lula ridicules Flávio Bolsonaro and says that 'there is no Vorcaro money here'" and that the president stated that "Flávio Bolsonaro's tie to Vorcaro is a police matter". In **institutional regulation**, O Globo revisits that [TSE](/en/glossary#tse) has already banned exhibition of a film about Bolsonaro during elections — material that resurfaces in the context of the Dark Horse case. The BBC explains "Why the budget for a film about Bolsonaro is considered disproportionate". Sergio Moro (Pleno.News) defends Flávio Bolsonaro and launches criticism at [PT](/en/glossary#pt).

In **pre-candidate movements**, Brasil de Fato reports that "Ciro Gomes will launch pre-candidacy this Saturday (16) amid crisis involving Flávio Bolsonaro" — with support described from Bolsonarists. And on operational agenda: **collective campaign financing begins today, Friday May 15** (Agência Brasil, CNN Brasil, SBT News, Rádio Itatiaia, Conexão Tocantins, Montanhas Capixabas). O TEMPO sustains the complementary thesis: "Trump effect in the 2026 election boosts intention to vote for Lula and pressures Flávio Bolsonaro". Gazeta do Povo enumerates the polls that will measure post-audio electoral impact.## 4. Daily divergences

> **Market × research ([Datafolha](/en/glossary#datafolha)):** [Polymarket](/en/glossary#polymarket) prices a gap of +15.65pp Lula in the [1º turno](/en/glossary#primeiro-turno) on May 15, while [Datafolha published this Friday shows a gap of only +4pp (Lula 39 × Flávio 35)](https://revistaforum.com.br/politica/pesquisa-datafolha-flavio/) — identical to the reading of April 11, before the Flávio-Vorcaro audio. **Declared vote held**, market priced in sustained erosion. The two readings can coexist: research captures stimulated intention (Flávio remains a stable nominal alternative), market prices the probability of reaching the election in October (Flávio under pressure from Centrão and Tebet questioning viability).

> **Research × research:** [Datafolha](/en/glossary#datafolha) May 15 (field May 12–14, post-Vorcaro complete) shows 2nd round Flávio 46 × Lula 45 ([empate técnico](/en/glossary#empate-tecnico)). Vox Brasil May 15 (field prior to the Vorcaro case according to Bahia Notícias) has Flávio 43.8 × Lula 40.2 (Flávio further ahead). [AtlasIntel](/en/glossary#atlasintel) tracking May 15 (coverage by Exame and VEJA) indicates Lula 7pp ahead in the [2º turno](/en/glossary#segundo-turno) post-audios. The difference between Vox and [Datafolha](/en/glossary#datafolha) is explained by the field period — Vox does not capture the Vorcaro effect. The difference between [AtlasIntel](/en/glossary#atlasintel) and [Datafolha](/en/glossary#datafolha) may reflect big-data online methodology capturing the digital effect more quickly.

> **[STF](/en/glossary#stf) impeach × institutional event:** the market for impeachment of an [STF](/en/glossary#stf) justice moved from 4.50% on May 14 at 09h to 8.00% at the close of Friday and surges to 12.75% on May 15 at 11h — an increase of 8.25pp in 24 hours. **There is no new institutional event today** justifying the spike (no new [STF](/en/glossary#stf) decision, no new amnesty [PEC](/en/glossary#pec) on the agenda). The observational reading: the market may be repricing the secondary effect of Vorcaro+Centrão revisiting Flávio (which sustained the decline in the impeachment market via politics in May) or it may be noise from low liquidity. The next [Datafolha](/en/glossary#datafolha) in June and [AtlasIntel](/en/glossary#atlasintel) tracking will measure whether the sustenance is structural.

## In summary

1. **The [Datafolha](/en/glossary#datafolha) published on May 15 (n=2.004, field May 12–14)** is the first large national research with complete field already post-Flávio-Vorcaro audio. The [1º turno](/en/glossary#primeiro-turno) repeats Lula 39 × Flávio 35 — identical to the [Datafolha](/en/glossary#datafolha) of April 11. The [2º turno](/en/glossary#segundo-turno) maintains [empate técnico](/en/glossary#empate-tecnico) (Lula 45 × Flávio 46). Stimulated declared vote held; Flávio's unaided awareness rose (12→16%) signaling more salience without changing the central reading.

2. **[Polymarket](/en/glossary#polymarket) priced in Flávio's erosion in greater magnitude** than [Datafolha](/en/glossary#datafolha) registers. Lula expands to 44.50% (↑1.50pp), Flávio yields to 28.85% (↓4.15pp), gap reaches +15.65pp Lula (vs +10pp at the May 14 close and +4pp in [Datafolha](/en/glossary#datafolha) 1st round). [STF impeach surges to 12.75% (↑4.75pp in 18h, ↑8.25pp in 24h)](https://polymarket.com/event/any-brazil-stf-justice-removed-by-impeachment-before-2027) with no triggering institutional event. Market reads a trajectory of sustained pressure that has not yet materialized in declared intention.

3. **Political coordination intensifies** with Centrão rethinking alliance (Folha), Tebet (G1) and The Economist (BBC) questioning the viability of the candidacy, BBC arguing that "voters looked to the side" toward Caiado and Zema without benefiting Lula, Lula joking "there's no Vorcaro money here" (Folha), [TSE](/en/glossary#tse) resuming the ban on displaying a film about Bolsonaro during elections (O Globo), Ciro Gomes launching a pre-candidacy on Saturday (Brasil de Fato) and crowdfunding beginning today (Agência Brasil, CNN Brasil). Next polls Veritá on May 16 (n=3.330), [AtlasIntel](/en/glossary#atlasintel) ×2 on May 19 (n=1.200+5.000) and VETOR ARROW on May 20 (n=9.000) will measure whether the market × [Datafolha](/en/glossary#datafolha) divergence reconciles in some direction.—

- [Gazeta do Povo — The surveys that will measure electoral impact after audio between Flávio Bolsonaro and Daniel Vorcaro (May 15)](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMi-?oc=5)- [O TEMPO — Trump effect in 2026 election boosts voting intention for Lula and pressures Flávio Bolsonaro (May 15)](https://news.google.?oc=5)

**Method.** [Polymarket](/en/glossary#polymarket) cross-reference (live pricing via gamma API — Brazil presidential, 2nd place, 3rd place, [STF](/en/glossary#stf) impeachment, Senate, 2026 inflation), electoral polls registered with [TSE](/en/glossary#tse) ([Datafolha](/en/glossary#datafolha) BR-00290/2026, [AtlasIntel](/en/glossary#atlasintel) tracking, Vox Brasil) and daily journalistic coverage. Variations in percentage points compared to the snapshot from May 14, 17:30 BRT (close), except where another reference is noted. Each factual claim appears with an inline link to the primary source or plain-text attribution to the outlet. Synthesis generated by AFOS Analytics and reviewed before publication.
